qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: execute_python_file allows shell injection via crafted .py filenames despite EXECUTE_LOCAL_COMMANDS=False
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets shell injection in the legacy `execute_python_file` helper under `autogpt/commands/execute_code.py`. The advisory instead describes missing `disabled`-flag enforcement on authenticated AutoGPT Platform block-execution API endpoints, allowing execution of the disabled `BlockInstallationBlock` by UUID in `autogpt_platform/backend/...`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing `disabled` check on AutoGPT Platform block-execution endpoints or execution of the disabled `BlockInstallationBlock` UUID.
evidence: The advisory root cause is the absence of `obj.disabled` enforcement in `autogpt_platform/backend/backend/api/features/v1.py` and `autogpt_platform/backend/backend/api/external/v1/routes.py`, which lets an authenticated user execute disabled block `45e78db5-03e9-447f-9395-308d712f5f08` and reach `BlockInstallationBlock` RCE.

The only final qualifying finding is `execute_python_file_command_injection`, which cites `autogpt/commands/execute_code.py`, `autogpt/app.py`, `autogpt/prompt.py`, and `.env.template` and reproduces shell injection through a crafted local `.py` filename.

Session artifact `artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` scopes the analyzed release surface to the legacy `autogpt/` runtime package and names `execute_python_file` as the primary retained finding; it does not identify the advisory's authenticated block-execution API or disabled-block path.
